revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Atsuhitakahiko-jinja]][[ja:熱日高彦神社]] {{Expand Japanese|熱日高彦神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Atsuhitakahiko Shrine | native_name = 熱日高彦神社 | image = Haiden of Atsuhitakahiko-jinja shrine.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Atsuhitakahiko Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|37.93772|140.832942}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|de|Ninigi|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|eo|Ninigi|es|Ninigi no Mikoto|fa|نینیگی-نو-میکوتو|fr|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|id|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|it|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニニギ|ko|니니기|mni|ꯅꯤꯅꯤꯒꯤ-ꯅꯣ-ꯃꯤꯀꯣꯇꯣ|nl|Ninigi|no|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|pl|Ninigi|pt|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ru|Ниниги но Микото|simple|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|sv|Ninigi|th|นินิงิ โนะ มิโกโตะ|tl|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|tr|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|uk|Нініґі но мікото|vi|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|zh|瓊瓊杵尊|qid=Q1056017|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} }} {{nihongo|'''Atsuhitakahiko Shrine'''|熱日高彦神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Q134917286|ltq=Q134917286|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11658590|ltq=Q11658590|qid=Q11658590|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province}} ** Series ordinal: 96 ** P155: {{ill|Q11447922|ltq=Q11447922|qid=Q11447922|1=Kobimine Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Toriyamineno Shrine|lt=Toriyamineno Shrine|qid=Q135040048|1=Toriyamineno Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q696425|ltq=Q696425|qid=Q696425|1=Kakuda, Miyagi}} * {{ill|Q907389|ltq=Q907389|qid=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}} * {{ill|Igu District, Miyagi|lt=Igu district|ceb|Igu-gun|en|Igu District, Miyagi|es|Distrito de Igu|fa|شهرستان ایگو، میاگی|fr|District d'Igu|id|Distrik Igu, Miyagi|it|Distretto di Igu|ja|伊具郡|ko|이구군|pl|Powiat Igu|ru|Игу|sr|Област Игу|uk|Повіт Іґу|zh_yue|伊具郡|zh|伊具郡|qid=Q1155531|1=Igu District, Miyagi}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|37.93772|140.832942}} == image (P18) == * Haiden of Atsuhitakahiko-jinja shrine.JPG == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|de|Ninigi|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|eo|Ninigi|es|Ninigi no Mikoto|fa|نینیگی-نو-میکوتو|fr|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|id|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|it|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニニギ|ko|니니기|mni|ꯅꯤꯅꯤꯒꯤ-ꯅꯣ-ꯃꯤꯀꯣꯇꯣ|nl|Ninigi|no|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|pl|Ninigi|pt|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ru|Ниниги но Микото|simple|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|sv|Ninigi|th|นินิงิ โนะ มิโกโตะ|tl|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|tr|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|uk|Нініґі но мікото|vi|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|zh|瓊瓊杵尊|qid=Q1056017|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182133 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Commons category (P373) == * Atsuhitakahiko-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '宮城県角田市島田字四拾刈12', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Atsuhitakahiko-jinja}} [[JA:熱日高彦神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11569065}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Atsuhitakahiko Shrine |Image=[[File:Haiden of Atsuhitakahiko-jinja shrine.JPG|270px]]
Worship hall |Location={{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|qid=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kakuda City|Q696425|qid=Q696425|1=Kakuda, Miyagi}} Shimada aza Yonjūgari 12 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|qid=Q1056017|lt=Amatsuhidaka Hikobanono Ninigi no Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
{{ill|lt=Igu District|Q1155531|qid=Q1155531|1=Igu District, Miyagi}} Sōsha |Founded=(Traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} |Main hall={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Annual festival={{ill|lt=April 6|Q2506|qid=Q2506|1=April 6}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Atsuhi
Takahiko Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Atsuhitakahiko Shrine''' (あつひたかひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Kakuda City|Q696425|qid=Q696425|1=Kakuda, Miyagi}}, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|qid=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=shrine listed in the Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and also serves as the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|qid=Q1107129|lt=general shrine|1=Sōja shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Igu District|Q1155531|qid=Q1155531|1=Igu District, Miyagi}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|qid=Q1056017|lt=Amatsuhidaka Hikonohono Ninigi no Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} ** In addition, the shrine also jointly enshrines Susanoo no Mikoto, Shiotsuchi no Oji no Mikoto, Ōnamuchi no Kami, Sukunahikona no Mikoto, and the deities of nearby shrines such as Shirayama Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Suijin Shrine, and Tachi Inari Shrine. === History === According to shrine tradition, Yamato Takeru, a son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}, was ordered by imperial command to head east. On his journey, he reached the land of Igu and established a ritual site at the foot of Mount Ōmori, an ancient sacred place, where he enshrined Ninigi no Mikoto, the ancestral deity of the Yamato imperial court. Afterward, Yamato Takeru set out on his return to the capital, but upon his death, Emperor Keikō, in remembrance of his son’s achievements, ordered the construction of a shrine building and enshrined Yamato Takeru together with Ninigi no Mikoto. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, Atsuhitakahiko Shrine was listed as one of the hundred Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province. According to the Igu District Gazetteer, the sacred object of Atsuhitakahiko Shrine is said to be a bronze Buddhist statue cast at Nyoiji Temple in Yamashiro Province by order of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|qid=Q329753|lt=the Engi Emperor|1=Emperor Daigo}}. During the Edo period, Atsuhitakahiko Shrine was regarded as the sōsha of Igu District. About 4 km west of the shrine are the ruins of the Igu District government office, known as the Kōriyama archaeological site. In the 5th year of the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era (1872), it was designated the only gōsha within Igu District. At the spring grand festival, in the past, the portable shrines (mikoshi) of Kashima Shrine in Kosai, Marumori Town (enshrining {{ill|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi no Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}), and Katori Shrine in the Ōyama-Ōtani area of Kakuda City (enshrining {{ill|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi no Kami|Q1107286|qid=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}) would gather at Atsuhitakahiko Shrine, and the three mikoshi would proceed together in a joint procession. At present, the procession is held jointly with the mikoshi of Katori Shrine only. File:Haiden of Katori-jinja shrine.JPG|Ōtani Katori Shrine File:Haiden of Kosai Kashima-jinja shrine.JPG|Kosai Kashima Shrine Atsuhitakahiko Shrine is revered as a shrine bestowing divine blessings for fertility and childrearing, martial arts and politics, fire prevention, agriculture, and traffic safety. === Precincts === File:Main approach to Atsuhitakahiko-jinja shrine.JPG|Stairway on the approach to Atsuhitakahiko Shrine File:Atsuhitakahiko-jinja shrine.JPG|Approach to Atsuhitakahiko Shrine File:Precincts of Atsuhitakahiko-jinja shrine.JPG|Precincts of Atsuhitakahiko Shrine * Shrine buildings (worship hall, offering hall, main hall) File:Haiden_of_Atsuhitakahiko-jinja_shrine_in_Summer.jpg|Worship hall of Atsuhitakahiko Shrine in summer File:Honden of Atsuhitakahiko-jinja shrine.JPG|Main hall of Atsuhitakahiko Shrine * Shirayamahime Shrine (enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Kikurihime no Kami|Q11619891|qid=Q11619891|1=Kikurihime}}, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi no Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=Izanami}}, {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuhanome no Kami}}) * Komayumine Inari Shrine File:Shirayamahime-jinja shrine of Atsuhitakahiko-jinja shrine.JPG|Shirayamahime Shrine File:Komayumine Inari-jinja shrine of Atsuhitakahiko-jinja shrine.JPG|Komayumine Inari Shrine === References === {{No footnotes|date=August 2020|section=1}} * “Guidebook for Worship at Atsuhitakahiko Shrine” (Atsuhitakahiko Shrine Office) * “Igu District Gazetteer” (1996, Igu District Education Association) * “The Oldest History of Tsunamis in Japan Revealed” {{ill|lt=Iinuma Yūgi|Q102246963|qid=Q102246963|1=Yūgi Iinuma}} 2013/03 {{ill|lt=Chōeisha|Q11674991|qid=Q11674991|1=Choeisha}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related articles === * {{ill|lt=Kurosukan|Q97341925|qid=Q97341925|1=Tohru Kurosu}} === External links === {{commonscat|Atsuhitakahiko-jinja}} * [http://hitaka.org/ Official website of Atsuhitakahiko Shrine] * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010232 Atsuhitakahiko Shrine] (Miyagi Prefecture {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q11590703|qid=Q11590703|1=Jinjachō}}) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha Prefecture]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kakuda]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kakuda
